nodes.context: Chinese triad formed by former Red Guards, active in Canada, Hong Kong, and globally.
Paramilitary organization established by Mao Zedong during the Cultural Revolution.
Transnational crime syndicate led by Tse Chi Lop, also known as The Company.
Chinese criminal gangs from Fujian province.

edges.evidence_quote: They were given the moniker "Big Circle Gang" and were formed by former members of the Red Guards
edges.context: Big Circle Gang was formed by former members of the Red Guards.

edges.evidence_quote: the Big Circle Boys in Toronto under the leadership of Tse Chi Lop ... was said to have formed an alliance with the Vito Rizzuto, the boss of Montreal's Rizzuto family
edges.context: Big Circle Gang formed an alliance with the Rizzuto family.

edges.evidence_quote: the Big Circle Boys in Toronto under the leadership of Tse Chi Lop ... was said to have formed an alliance with ... the Columbian Cali Cartel
edges.context: Big Circle Gang formed an alliance with the Cali Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: the Big Circle Boys in Toronto under the leadership of Tse Chi Lop ... was said to have formed an alliance with ... the 'Ndrangheta
edges.context: Big Circle Gang formed an alliance with the 'Ndrangheta.

edges.evidence_quote: Tse Chi Lop, alleged to have assumed the leadership of the organisation and having taken it to higher levels as Sam Gor (The Company)
edges.context: Big Circle Gang evolved into or is linked to Sam Gor under Tse Chi Lop.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Company, American Mafia, Red Wa, Fujian gangs, Wo Shing Wo, Menace of Destruction, Asian Boyz, MS-13, Netas
edges.context: Big Circle Gang lists American Mafia as an ally.
